title: 10 best herbal creams for itchy skin

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with itchy skin because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb contains compounds like calendula, which have natural analgesic and antipruritic effects that calm irritated skin and reduce itching. Additionally, pot marigold's moisturizing qualities help to lock in moisture, alleviating dryness and flakiness that can exacerbate itchiness. By applying a pot marigold cream to itchy areas, individuals can experience immediate relief from discomfort and enjoy smoother, healthier-looking skin over time.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Pot marigold cream's antihistamine properties help to reduce itching and inflammation by blocking the release of histamine, a chemical that causes allergic reactions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with itchy skin conditions.

Emollient properties: Pot marigold cream's emollient properties, which come from its high content of fatty acids and glycerin, help to soothe and moisturize dry, itchy skin by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good at soothing and calming itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory properties, making them an effective ingredient in creams.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help reduce inflammation and itching, making them a popular choice for natural skincare products.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers, ensuring at least 2 ounces of dried or 4 cups of fresh petals for the recipe.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried calendula petals with 8 fluid ounces of warm carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until it reaches a rich yellow color.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.

Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of vitamin e oil with the infused oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and whisking to create a smooth consistency, allowing it to cool and solidify before using as a topical cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with itchy skin because of their exceptional soothing properties. The gel inside the aloe vera leaf contains anti-inflammatory agents that reduce redness and swelling, calming irritated skin. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing qualities help to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and flakiness that can exacerbate itchiness. This natural combination effectively relieves itching sensations, providing instant relief for itchy skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and even insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloenin and anthraquinones, help reduce inflammation and relieve itching caused by irritated skin.
Moisturizing properties: Herbal aloe creams' moisturizing properties soothe dry, itchy skin by providing long-lasting hydration, reducing discomfort and flaking.
Antioxidant activity: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from environmental stressors and oxidative damage, which can contribute to itchiness and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Containing aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help relieve itching and irritation caused by skin conditions.
Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate itchy skin symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including aloe vera gel, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil in the following proportions.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over simmering water for 10 minutes.
Mix aloe vera gel with coconut oil and vitamin e oil in a separate bowl until well combined using a spatula.
Add the melted beeswax to the aloe vera mixture and stir until smooth using an immersion blender or whisk.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as beta-carotene and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation and itching caused by conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of stinging nettle creams help lock in moisture, leaving skin feeling soft, smooth, and supple. This natural cream provides instant relief from itchy skin, promoting a healthy and comfortable complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Stinging nettle cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin conditions like eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis.
Antihistamine effect: The cream's antihistamine properties block the release of histamines, which are responsible for itching and inflammation in response to allergens or irritants.

Soil soothing and moisturizing: Stinging nettle cream's rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as fatty acids, making it a potent skin soother that helps repair and moisturize dry, itchy skin while reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, nettle roots help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and vitamins, nettle seeds moisturize and nourish dry, irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves, carefully handling the plant to avoid irritating skin.
Dry the collected leaves in a warm, dry environment for at least 24 hours to preserve potency.
Blend one part dried stinging nettle leaves with four parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for two hours, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain and filter the infused oil, then whip in beeswax and essential oils to create a soothing cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The cream's main ingredient, allantoin, soothes irritated skin and reduces redness, providing quick relief from itching and scratching. Additionally, comfrey contains triterpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help to calm the skin and reduce inflammation caused by itchy conditions such as eczema and psoriasis. As a result, comfrey creams provide effective long-term relief for itchy skin, promoting healthy skin regeneration and reducing the risk of further irritation.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey cream contains allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes itchy skin by reducing redness and swelling, allowing for faster healing.
Glucomannan: Glucomannan, a type of fiber found in comfrey, helps to lock in moisture and provide a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing dryness and itchiness.
Alkamides: Alkamides present in comfrey cream have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce skin irritation and inflammation caused by itching, promoting healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing and calming itchy skin.
Buds: Comfrey buds contain a high concentration of allantoin, a natural ingredient that promotes skin healing and reduces inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried symphytum officinale leaves, 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax.
Infuse the dried symphytum officinale leaves in the sweet almond oil for two hours under low heat stirring occasionally.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl, squeezing the leaves to extract as much oil as possible.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of aloe vera gel to the infused oil mixture, mixing well until combined.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax is melted, stirring occasionally, then pour into sterilized jars for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple coneflower cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce itchiness and redness caused by skin irritation, making it an effective treatment for conditions like eczema and psoriasis.
Antimicrobial: Purple coneflower cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial, fungal, and viral infections that can cause itchy skin, such as athlete's foot or ringworm.
Soothing and Calming: Purple coneflower cream's soothing and calming properties provide relief from itching by reducing histamine release, which can contribute to intense itching sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing itchy skin.
Barks: The barks of the purple coneflower contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and itching, as well as provide antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried echinacea purpurea flowers from a trusted source and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the powdered echinacea with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar and let it infuse for 24 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the herbal solids and transfer the infused oil to a heat-resistant bowl.
Add 20g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil to the infused oil and stir until the mixture reaches a temperature of 65°c for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature before use within six months.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with itchy skin because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called echinacea, which soothes and calms irritated skin. The cream's natural ingredients also help to reduce redness and swelling, providing quick relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties lock in hydration, further calming and soothing dry, itchy skin, leaving it feeling soft, smooth, and comforted.

Jesuit's bark creams helps with itchy skin because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active ingredients, derived from the inner bark of the Jesuit's tree, work to reduce itching and inflammation caused by various skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and allergic reactions. By calming the skin and reducing redness and irritation, Jesuit's bark creams provide quick relief from itchy skin, allowing individuals to feel comfortable and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Quinine in cinchona bark can reduce inflammation and itching by inhibiting the release of histamine and other pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antipruritic properties: The quinoline alkaloids present in cinchona bark have been shown to have an antipruritic effect, meaning they can help relieve itching sensations without causing significant sedation or other adverse effects.

Antimicrobial properties: Cinchona bark contains compounds that exhibit antimicrobial activity against various types of bacteria and fungi, which can help prevent infection and reduce inflammation associated with itchy skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Jesuit's Bark plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming itchy skin.
Barks: The barks of the Jesuit's Bark plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of dried cinchona officinalis leaves and crush them into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for ten minutes.
Combine the crushed cinchona officinalis with three tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep for four hours, then strain it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax and one tablespoon of vitamin e oil to the infused liquid, stirring until the beeswax has melted completely.
Pour the cream into small tin containers, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The natural extract of chamomile flowers contains apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces redness and swelling, providing instant relief from itchiness. Additionally, the cream's emollient texture provides a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing dryness, making it an excellent remedy for alleviating itching caused by eczema, psoriasis, or other skin conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation and alleviate itching caused by conditions like eczema, dermatitis, and sunburn.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which exacerbates skin irritation and itchiness, allowing the skin to heal faster and reducing inflammation.
Soothing property: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have a calming effect on the skin, providing instant relief from itching and discomfort by reducing skin redness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing apigenin and other bioactive compounds, chamomile roots help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and inflammation.
Flowers: Rich in azulene and other flavonoids, chamomile flowers have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in calming itchy skin and promoting wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine 10 grams of dried chamomile flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm and dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax pellets and 10 milliliters of pure jojoba oil in a double boiler, heating until the beeswax is fully melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with itchy skin because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as rosmarinic acid and triterpenoids, work to reduce redness and swelling associated with itching, providing instant relief from irritation. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects soothe the skin, reducing the urge to scratch and allowing the skin to heal faster. As a result, herbal lemon balm creams offer effective and gentle relief for itchy skin conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lemon balm cream's antihistamine properties help to reduce itching and inflammation by blocking the release of histamine, a chemical released in response to allergens or irritants.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid, help to soothe and calm itchy skin by reducing redness, swelling, and pain.

Antiviral properties: Lemon balm's antiviral properties can help to combat viral infections that cause itchiness, such as herpes simplex or shingles, by inhibiting the growth of viruses and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemon balm roots can help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Leaves: Packed with vitamin C and other nutrients, lemon balm leaves have antiseptic and anti-itch properties that make them ideal for creating creams to alleviate skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh melissa officinalis leaves and 1 cup of sweet almond oil.
Chop the melissa leaves into small pieces and combine with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until the wax is melted.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before transferring to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce the itchiness by calming the skin and soothing irritated areas. Additionally, St John's Wort has natural antimicrobial properties that help to combat any underlying infections or fungal issues that may be contributing to the itchiness. As a result, the cream provides fast and effective relief from itchy skin, allowing individuals to enjoy comfortable and peaceful skin once again.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help to reduce itchiness and discomfort by reducing redness and swelling.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate itchy skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic effects, meaning it can directly help to reduce itching sensations by blocking histamine receptors in the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help relieve itching and irritation.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in volatile oils, particularly hyperforin and adhyperforin, which have analgesic and antipruritic effects, providing relief from itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hypericum perforatum flowers and mix them with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:4.
Combine the mixture in a double boiler and heat it for 30 minutes at low temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add beeswax and coconut oil to the liquid and stir until fully incorporated, then pour into containers for storage.
Allow the cream to cool and harden completely before using it on itchy skin, taking approximately 30 minutes preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbs in dandelion cream, such as calendula and chamomile, calm the skin and reduce redness and irritation, providing quick relief from itchiness. Additionally, dandelion cream's moisturizing ingredients help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy patches of skin, leaving skin feeling soft and comfortable. By addressing the root causes of itchiness, dandelion creams offer a natural and effective solution for those seeking relief from itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce itching and swelling caused by irritated skin, making it a effective remedy for conditions like eczema and psoriasis.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin irritation and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing itchiness.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Dandelion cream's soothing and moisturizing properties help to calm and hydrate the skin, providing instant relief from dry, itchy, and irritated skin conditions like atopic dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help relieve itchiness and skin irritation.
Leaves: Contain salicylic acid, a beta hydroxy acid that helps exfoliate and unclog pores, reducing itching and inflammation caused by skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 20 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of coconut oil, and 5 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright creams helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal extract soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort caused by itchiness. Additionally, eyebright's astringent properties help to tighten the skin's pores, which can reduce moisture loss and alleviate dryness. As a result, eyebright creams provide fast and effective relief from itchy skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright creams contain flavonoids and terpenes that reduce inflammation, which helps to alleviate itching and discomfort caused by irritated skin.

Antiseptic and antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in eyebright cream help to prevent infections and reduce the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses on the skin, reducing itchiness and promoting healing.
Soothing and calming properties: Eyebright creams contain astringent and emollient properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching, redness, and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Eyebright roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy skin.
Seeds: Eyebright seeds contain fatty acids and vitamins that help soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried euphrasia officinalis leaves in sufficient quantity for processing.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours or until completely dry.
Grind the dried euphrasia officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with distilled water to create a 10% infusion, steeping for 2 hours before straining.
Combine the herbal infusion with coconut oil and beeswax in a ratio of 3:1, heating until the mixture reaches 60°c and solidifies into a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and calm irritated skin, providing quick relief from itchy skin symptoms. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to alleviate itchy skin discomfort, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation for those suffering from itchy skin conditions.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and itching by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators, such as histamine and bradykinin.
Flavonoids: Possesses anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to soothe and calm itchy skin, reducing redness and swelling.
Saponins: Exhibits antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent infections that can exacerbate itchy skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness and inflammation.
Barks: Containing compounds like licoricidin and licorisoflavan A, which have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties to alleviate itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20% glycerin and 30% distilled water to prepare a base solution taking 10 minutes.
Infuse 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in boiling water for 30 minutes to create a tea-like extract.
Combine the base solution with the infused glycyrrhiza glabra root extract and stir until it thickens over 60 minutes at room temperature.
Mix 2% beeswax and 1% coconut oil with the resulting mixture and heat gently at 70°c for 15 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken further before filling into jars and storing in a refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream is made from the leaves of the plantain plant, which contains antioxidants and flavonoids that help to calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation. When applied topically, the cream forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing dryness, while also providing gentle relief from itching and scratching. This natural remedy provides long-lasting comfort and relief for itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with itchiness.

Antipruritic: The antipruritic properties of plantain creams help to reduce itching sensations by blocking the release of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions that contributes to itching.
Soothing and moisturizing: Plantain creams contain emollients and humectants that lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing itchiness and reducing redness.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain tannins and phenolic acids, which help to calm itching and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves in the early morning and dry them thoroughly within 24 hours for optimal potency.
Grind dried plantago major leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder and sift through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps.
Mix 30 grams of powdered plantago major with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 20 minutes.
Add 15 grams of sweet almond oil and stir until the mixture reaches a smooth, creamy consistency, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized jars, label, and store them in the refrigerator to maintain potency for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato creams helps with itchy skin because they contain a unique blend of natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb extract in these creams has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching, making them an effective treatment for conditions such as eczema and psoriasis. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the wild potato help to lock in moisture, leaving skin feeling soft and supple. As a result, herbal wild potato creams provide long-lasting relief from itchy skin symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wild potato creams contain anti-inflammatory agents that reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations and conditions like eczema.
Antioxidant activity: Herbal wild potato creams exhibit antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to itchy skin symptoms.

Soothing and emollient effects: The natural extracts in wild potato creams have a calming effect on the skin, providing moisture and hydration while soothing dryness and irritation, reducing itching and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild potato plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems that connect roots to shoots, have natural antiseptic properties that prevent infection and reduce itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum kok saghyz roots and dry them in a warm place for 7 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and set aside 20 grams.
Mix the powdered root with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to remove any impurities and transfer the infused oil to a clean glass container.
Add 50 milliliters of beeswax and stir until melted, then pour the cream into small tin containers and let cool at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry creams helps with itchy skin because of its unique blend of natural ingredients that soothe and calm irritated skin. The anti-inflammatory properties in blueberries help reduce redness and swelling, while the cream's moisturizing properties hydrate dry skin, relieving itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, antioxidants in the cream work to combat free radicals, which can cause skin irritation and inflammation, leaving skin feeling smooth, soft, and calm. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief from itchy skin without harsh chemicals or artificial fragrances.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Blueberries contain high levels of antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress on the skin, thereby alleviating itchiness and soothing irritated skin.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal blueberry creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with itchy skin conditions.
Moisturizing properties: Blueberries are rich in moisturizing compounds like anthocyanins, which can help to hydrate the skin, reducing itchiness caused by dryness or dehydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Blueberry roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing itchy skin and reducing redness.
Fruits: Blueberry fruits contain anthocyanins and flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects that help calm itching and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of vaccinium myrtillus berries and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Combine the berries with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed jojoba oil and let it macerate for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of distilled water to the liquid extract, stirring until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a double boiler and heat it gently for 30 minutes, or until the cream reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams help with itchy skin because of their soothing and calming properties. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in damask roses work to reduce redness and irritation, providing instant relief from itchiness. The cream's moisturizing qualities also help to lock in hydration, which can be particularly beneficial for dry or irritated skin. As a result, damask rose creams provide a natural and gentle solution for soothing itchy skin, making them an excellent option for those seeking a non-invasive and chemical-free treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Damask rose creams contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that reduce inflammation, which can cause itching and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in damask rose cream neutralize free radicals, protecting the skin from damage caused by environmental stressors and reducing the appearance of itchy skin.
Soothing and calming properties: The mucilages and saponins present in damask rose cream have a cooling effect on the skin, providing instant relief from itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots of the damask rose plant help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids and phenolic acids, flowers of the damask rose plant have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena flowers at dawn or early morning when they are most fragrant.
Dry the flowers in small batches using a clean and well-ventilated area taking 2 weeks.
Infuse the dried flowers in sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 10:5 for 2 weeks stirring daily.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil at a ratio of 70:15:15 heating gently until melted then pouring into jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with itchy skin because of their ability to soothe and calm irritated skin. The herbs used in these creams, such as aloe vera and chamomile, possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling associated with itching. Additionally, elder creams often contain moisturizing ingredients like coconut oil and shea butter that help to lock in moisture and hydrate the skin, further reducing itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder cream contains antioxidants like flavonoids, anthocyanins, and polyphenols that neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, which can contribute to itchiness.

Soothing and Calming effects: The herbal extracts in elder cream, such as chamomile and calendula, have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and discomfort.

Moisturizing and Protective barrier formation: Elder cream's emollient and humectant ingredients, like glycerin and panthenol, lock in moisture, forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, which can help alleviate dryness and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming itchy skin.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and relieve itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in mid-summer for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or low heat for 7 days to preserve essential oils.
Combine 20% sambucus nigra dried flower and leaf mixture with 80% coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Steep the mixture at 180°f for 30 minutes to infuse the oil with bioactive compounds from the plants.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into clean containers within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut creams helps with itchy skin because they contain aescin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties. Aescin reduces inflammation and itching by blocking the release of histamine, a chemical mediator involved in allergic reactions. Additionally, horse chestnut extracts have moisturizing effects, soothing dry and irritated skin while locking in moisture. As a result, horse chestnut creams provide fast relief from itchy skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and insect bites, allowing for comfortable and smooth skin.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin, a saponin extracted from horse chestnut, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in horse chestnut creams have antihistaminic and anti-allergic effects, which can help to reduce the body's response to allergens and alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with allergic skin reactions.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids found in horse chestnut creams have antimicrobial properties that can help to combat bacterial, fungal, or parasitic infections that may contribute to itchy skin conditions such as acne, impetigo, or ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Horse chestnut roots contain a high concentration of aesculin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm itchy skin.
Seeds: The seeds of the horse chestnut plant are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which work together to moisturize and protect dry, itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 20 grams of dried aesculus hippocastanum bark and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating slowly over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated, then continue heating for an additional 15 minutes or until melted completely.
Remove the mixture from heat and let cool slightly before adding 10 milliliters of liquid lanolin, stirring well to combine; allow to cool to room temperature over 2 hours.
Whip the cooled mixture using a hand blender or whisk for 5 minutes, then pour into sterilized containers and store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly creams helps with itchy skin because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbs used in these creams, such as oatmeal and chamomile, work to calm irritated skin and reduce redness. Additionally, the creams often feature ingredients like aloe vera and tea tree oil, which possess antiseptic and antimicrobial qualities that help to combat infections and promote healthy skin. As a result, herbal dog holly creams provide long-lasting relief for itchy skin, allowing dogs to enjoy a more comfortable and peaceful life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal dog holly creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin allergies and irritations.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in herbal dog holly creams work to neutralize free radicals, which can damage the skin and exacerbate itchy skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.

Soil-acting agents: Herbal dog holly creams contain soothing and calming ingredients that help to normalize the skin's natural barrier function, reducing itching and discomfort caused by dry, irritated skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are used to reduce itching and irritation caused by allergic reactions or insect bites.
Barks: Containing alkaloids with antibacterial properties, barks help soothe and calm skin irritations, reducing itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried ruscus aculeatus leaves and 1 ounce of coconut oil and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried ruscus aculeatus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ground ruscus aculeatus powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 6 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax melts and forms a smooth, creamy consistency in 20 minutes.
Remove the cream from the double boiler and let it cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage at ambient temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden creams helps with itchy skin because they contain anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's natural ingredients, such as camphor and menthol, work together to reduce itching and inflammation, providing instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to hydrate the skin, leaving it feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed. This results in a significant reduction in itchiness and discomfort, making it an effective solution for individuals suffering from dry, itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream contains flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce redness and swelling associated with itching skin.

Soothing astringents: Tannins in Littleleaf Linden flowers and leaves act as astringents, constricting blood vessels to reduce itching and inflammation while also helping to dry up excessive moisture.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: The cream's antioxidant compounds help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, while its antimicrobial properties combat infections that can cause or exacerbate itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from itching.
Barks: The barks have natural antibacterial properties that help prevent infection and promote healing of skin irritations, reducing itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry tilia cordata leaves and flowers in shaded areas for 2 weeks to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind 20 grams of dried tilia cordata material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine the ground tilia cordata powder with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 1 hour.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized jars and seal tightly, storing them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freezing for up to 1 year.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for itchy skin are topical products that combine natural ingredients from plants, flowers, and herbs to soothe and calm irritated skin. These creams work by reducing inflammation, moisturizing dry patches, and providing a barrier against environmental irritants, ultimately relieving itching and discomfort. Examples of herbal creams that help with itchy skin include aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile-based products, which can be especially beneficial for individuals suffering from eczema, psoriasis, or skin allergies. By using these creams, people can enjoy relief from constant itching, allowing them to sleep better, wear comfortable clothing without discomfort, and participate in daily activities with confidence.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy skin with herbal creams is to identify the root cause of the itchiness. Then, apply a topical cream containing herbs like calendula, chamomile, or lavender, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Apply the cream regularly, ideally 2-3 times a day, until the itching subsides. Additionally, you can also mix the herbal cream with other natural ingredients like coconut oil or oatmeal to enhance its effectiveness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy skin that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and rosacea. Herbal creams can provide relief from symptoms such as redness, inflammation, and dryness associated with these conditions. For example, creams containing aloe vera, chamomile, and lavender have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can calm irritated skin, while creams infused with tea tree oil and calendula have antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help combat infections and reduce itching.
